decisions.reasoning: WWDC 2026 triggered classic sell-the-news drop of ~4% after Apple revealed revamped Siri AI (powered by Google Gemini) but gave NO launch timeline for features — investors expected September, got vague later-2026. Price broke below key $296 S1 pivot support (now $294.55); 15-min RSI at 26.65 is oversold suggesting short-term flush rather than structural break. Wedbush reaffirmed Outperform / $400 PT after WWDC. Apple revenue at $111.2B (strong). Daily trend structure (20-EMA > 50-EMA > 200-EMA) still intact. Holding through WWDC volatility; if price remains below $296 for 2+ more sessions or closes below 50-day MA ($288.62), will reassess trim.

sources.headline: Apple Launches Siri AI, Stock Drops 4%
Apple WWDC 2026: AAPL Stock Slips As Lack Of Launch Deadline For Siri AI Spooks Retail Investors
Apple stock tests key $296 support after WWDC AI hype fades

decisions.invalidation: Close below $288.62 (50-day MA), or continued multi-day rejection of $296 support, or evidence that Siri AI launch delayed beyond 2026

decisions.reasoning: SMA signal crossed to FLAT (bearish crossover: fast 214.13 just below slow 214.49) but fundamental thesis is extremely strong: FY2026 revenue $215.94B (+65% YoY), record $82B quarterly revenue, fastest Blackwell product ramp in company history. AI hyperscaler capex forecast to $1T in 2027. Average analyst target $298.42 (+43% from $208.10). June 5 drop of 6.2% appears to be noise rather than structural — stock recovered to $208 range. China revenue exclusion is a known headwind already baked in. Jensen Huang declining Senate testimony is not material to the business. Holding; the technical FLAT signal is a watch item but does not override the fundamental case.

decisions.invalidation: SMA stays flat or crosses bearish and price breaks below $200 round number; or material capex guidance cuts from hyperscalers (MSFT, GOOGL, META, AMZN)

decisions.reasoning: Strong fiscal Q3 2026 earnings beat on cloud and AI growth. $120M NHS England generative AI deal shows enterprise monetization continuing. FTC investigation into cloud/AI/bundling practices is an overhang but not new and not thesis-breaking. 52 Buy / 3 Hold / 0 Sell from 55 analysts; consensus target $522.53 (27% upside). Position is small (3 shares, $1,233 MV). No catalyst to act on today.

decisions.invalidation: FTC action escalates to structural remedy; Azure growth decelerates materially in next earnings print

decisions.reasoning: Only green position (+1.47%). Broad market outlook bullish: Citi 8100, Goldman 8000, Yardeni 8250 year-end targets for S&P 500. Earnings growth thesis driven by AI capex boom ($754B hyperscaler spend in 2026). Keeping as portfolio hedge / broad market exposure. Goldman Risk Appetite Indicator at 99th percentile is a caution flag — valuations are full — but no immediate catalyst for a major turn.

decisions.invalidation: Macro shock (Iran conflict escalation, recession signal), or S&P breaks below 200-day MA

decisions.reasoning: No new positions initiated. $701 reserve kept. Deploying capital into a -0.54% tape on the day of the WWDC miss (AAPL -4%) with Goldman Risk Appetite at 99th pct would be FOMO. No new thesis today warrants entering — monitoring QQQ as a potential add if tech stabilizes post-WWDC.

summary: First session: all 4 existing positions held (AAPL/MSFT/NVDA underwater, SPY green); AAPL WWDC disappointment is a watch item but oversold short-term; no new trades.
open_theses: AAPL: WWDC sell-the-news flush — monitor $296 support over next 2-3 days; Wedbush $400 target means thesis intact if support holds
NVDA: AI infrastructure capex supercycle intact; $298 analyst target; hold through FLAT SMA signal while fundamentals support
MSFT: Beat earnings, enterprise AI monetization in motion; hold for move toward $522 target
QQQ: Watching for post-WWDC tech stabilization as potential add if SPY/QQQ SMA signals remain long and cash can be deployed
